# Recommended Project Layout

Reference date: 2026-05-25.
Status: Current.

## Skeleton generated by `nucleus new`

`nucleus new` produces a **minimal skeleton**. The composition root is a
project-root `main.go` run with `go run .` — there is no `cmd/server/`
subdirectory. Both templates share the same base; the mvc template adds
`rbac_policy.csv`.

### api template

```text
myapp/
  main.go          ← nucleus.New().FromConfigFile("nucleus.yml").WithoutDefaults().Start()
  nucleus.yml
  .gitignore
  migrations/
  go.mod
  README.md
```

Serves only `/healthz`. No admin panel, no authz.

### mvc template

```text
myapp/
  main.go          ← nucleus.New().FromConfigFile("nucleus.yml").Start()
  nucleus.yml
  rbac_policy.csv  ← grants anonymous access to /healthz only
  .gitignore
  migrations/
  go.mod
  README.md
```

Serves `/healthz` and `/admin`. Default-deny Casbin policy; admin panel
active.

## Adding features as modules

The skeleton contains no domain code. Add features as `nucleus.Module` values
mounted at the composition root. See `examples/mvc_api/internal/notes` for a
complete worked example (model, REST Resource controller, migrations).

A typical project once features are added:

```text
myapp/
  main.go
  nucleus.yml
  rbac_policy.csv
  migrations/
  internal/
    notes/         ← one module per domain area
      module.go
      model.go
      controller.go
  go.mod
```

The layered architecture (controllers / services / repositories) is a valid
pattern you can structure modules with; it is not auto-scaffolded.

## Minimum to Start

1. `main.go` (project root)
2. `nucleus.yml`
3. `migrations/` (empty is fine until you add a model)

If background jobs are needed, add a `cmd/worker/main.go` entry point and
`internal/tasks/` — these are not generated by the scaffold.

If periodic tasks are needed, add a `cmd/scheduler/main.go` entry point —
also not generated by the scaffold.

## OpenAPI Contract Convention (experimental)

When you choose to add an OpenAPI contract lane, the recommended convention is:

1. place an `internal/contracts/contracts.go` package aggregator with `DefaultConfig()`, `NewDocument()`, and `NewDocumentWithConfig(cfg Config)`,
2. each per-resource contract file exposes `RegisterXContract(doc *openapi.Document)` and auto-registers with the aggregator,
3. `nucleus openapi --out openapi.json` and runtime serving use that same document builder,
4. use `pkg/openapi` helpers (`ObjectSchema`, `ArraySchema`, `DataEnvelopeSchema`, `CollectionEnvelopeSchema`, `IDSchema`, `JSONRequestBody`, `JSONResponse`, `ErrorResponse`, `EmptyResponse`, `PathParameter`, `QueryParameter`, `SearchQueryParameter`) to reduce repetition,
5. mount the document at runtime via `app.MountOpenAPI("/openapi.json", contracts.NewDocument)`.

This structure is not generated by `nucleus new`; add it when you are ready to publish an OpenAPI document.
